ONCOLOGY CLINICAL NOTES
Date: January 15, 2024
Patient: John Doe (ID: P12345)

CHIEF COMPLAINT:
Follow-up for metastatic colorectal cancer

HISTORY OF PRESENT ILLNESS:
65-year-old male with stage IV colorectal cancer, currently on FOLFOX chemotherapy.
Last imaging showed progression of disease.

PHYSICAL EXAMINATION:
Patient appears fatigued but in no acute distress.
Abdominal examination reveals mild tenderness in RLQ.

RECENT LAB RESULTS:
- CEA elevated at 8.5 ng/mL (up from 7.2 last month)
- CA 19-9 at 45 U/mL
- Tumor markers trending upward

IMAGING FINDINGS:
CT scan from today shows primary tumor now measuring 3.2 cm, increased from 2.8 cm on previous scan.
Multiple liver metastases, largest measuring 2.1 cm.

MOLECULAR TESTING:
EGFR mutation analysis positive - Exon 19 deletion detected.
Patient may be candidate for targeted therapy.

ASSESSMENT AND PLAN:
1. Disease progression despite current chemotherapy
2. Consider switch to EGFR-targeted therapy given mutation status
3. Discuss treatment options with patient and family
4. Repeat imaging in 6 weeks
5. Continue supportive care

Dr. Sarah Smith, MD
Medical Oncology
